mission.title: harbor: With the LDAP_AUTH or OIDC_AUTH, what if the current user is a group member and the group is granted as admin role?
mission.description: With the LDAP_AUTH or OIDC_AUTH, what if the current user is a group member and the group is granted as admin role?. This issue affects 9+ users.

steps.title: Identify harbor troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your harbor deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n harbor -l app.kubernetes.io/name=harbor
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=harbor -n harbor --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review harbor configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant harbor configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n harbor -l app.kubernetes.io/name=harbor
kubectl get configmap -n harbor -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=harbor
```
With the LDAP_AUTH or OIDC_AUTH, what if the current user is a group member and the group is granted as admin role?

_Originally posted by  in https://github.com/goharbor/harbor/pull/19543#discussion_r1385879526_
       
We ran into this issue.

steps.title: Upgrade Harbor to fix group-based role resolution
steps.description:
Upgrade Harbor to a version that correctly resolves group-based project roles:
```bash
# Update the Harbor Helm repo
helm repo update harbor

# Upgrade Harbor
helm upgrade harbor harbor/harbor -n harbor -f values.yaml

# Verify pods restart successfully
kubectl rollout status deployment/harbor-core -n harbor
```
After upgrading, users who belong to LDAP/OIDC groups with project admin roles will have full admin permissions on those projects.

steps.title: Confirm With the LDAP_AUTH or OIDC_AUTH, what if the… is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=harbor -n harbor --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n harbor --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary: When using LDAP or OIDC authentication, a user who is a member of a group granted project admin role cannot perform all admin actions because Harbor does not fully resolve group-based role memberships. The fix ensures that when checking user permissions, Harbor considers roles inherited through group membership in addition to directly assigned roles.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with harbor installed or the issue environment reproducible.
